sourceDesc.p: Produced from a Project Gutenberg HTML source using gutenberg-text-splitter.

body.div:
CHAPTER VI. Of the Logic of Induction.
Aphorism XVII. The Logic of Induction consists in stating the Facts and the Inference in such a manner, that the Evidence of the Inference is manifest: just as the Logic of Deduction consists in stating the Premises and the Conclusion in such a manner that the Evidence of the Conclusion is manifest.
